title: Global Internship — Season 19
description: An introduction to arts administration and production for students and recent graduates. Work directly with DAT staff and gain hands-on experience across theatre, eco-travel, and social impact.
long_description:
Build a connection with DAT's global team while getting hands-on experience in the arts, theatre, eco-travel, and social impact spaces. We are carefully selecting a small team of interns to join DAT during the season ahead — curious students and recent grads who want to make magic happen and see what running a global theatre company actually looks like.

You'll work on real projects with measurable outcomes. You'll be mentored by working artists and arts administrators. And — if you want it — you'll travel into the field with DAT for an ACTion expedition where the work gets out of the inbox and onto a stage.
what_youll_do:
Join a department of your choice — Producing, Development, Marketing, or Programs
Lead a small independent project tied to real-world goals
Attend weekly mentorship 1:1s with a DAT staff member
Participate in EDI training and professional-development workshops
Contribute to a team project alongside fellow PLXers
Optional: travel with DAT on an in-the-field ACTion expedition
who_you_are:
Curious, collaborative, bold, and scrappy
You take pride in what you make and how you make it
You love what you do and find yourself tinkering at strange hours
You are an ambitious go-getter who finishes what you start
You are a good soul — generous, kind, listening
requirements:
18 years or older
Able to commit to the 12-week program plus orientation weekend
Optional 4–6 week ACTion expedition abroad
Strong desire to learn within at least one DAT department
Reliable internet and a quiet space for video calls
perks:
Stipend: $1,800 USD
Travel grant: $1,200 USD (applied to ACTion expedition)
Academic credit available — email info@dramaticadventure.com
Free tickets to DAT events worldwide
Professional development trainings and EDI workshops
Lifetime DAT alumni network access
Letter of recommendation upon successful completion
timeline:
Application Deadline :: August 15, 2026
Orientation Weekend :: September 4–6, 2026 (remote)
Remote Work :: September 7 – November 28, 2026
Winter Break :: November 29 – January 3
Team Project :: January 4–24, 2027 (remote)
Optional ACTion Expedition :: February – May 2027
faq:
Do I need to be a theatre major? :: Nope. We've placed economics majors in Development and computer science majors in Production. We care about how you think, not what's on your transcript.
Is this remote-only? :: The core 12 weeks are remote. The ACTion expedition (optional but encouraged) is in-the-field — we've gone to Tanzania, Ecuador, Slovakia, and Czechia in recent seasons.
Can I do this for academic credit? :: Yes — email info@dramaticadventure.com and we'll work with your university.
What if I miss the deadline? :: Apply for the next cohort. We run PLX twice a year.

title: Global Apprenticeship — Season 19
description: An immersive paid training program for early-career arts administrators. Work as part of the team alongside DAT staff, attend professional development seminars, and gain in-the-field international experience.
compensation: $8,400 USD stipend + up to $8,000 travel grant
long_description:
A leadership development program for early-career arts administrators and theatre production practitioners. The Apprentice serves as a valued and instrumental member of the DAT staff for the season ahead.

Through hands-on experience, mentorship, and workshops, Apprentices are given the tools to become impactful leaders. We are carefully selecting one Apprentice to join DAT during the season ahead — someone ready to take real ownership of real work in a global arts organization.
what_youll_do:
Own a meaningful portfolio in Producing, Development, Marketing, or Programs
Lead an independent project with measurable impact across the season
Manage and mentor a small team of PLX Interns
Travel with DAT on at least one ACTion expedition
Attend two pro-dev retreat weekends with DAT staff and alumni mentors
Contribute to season-planning conversations as a member of the team
who_you_are:
1–3 years of arts admin, production, or nonprofit experience
Ambitious, scrappy, and entrepreneurial — you don't wait for permission
Action-oriented — you turn ideas into outcomes
Generous mentor energy — you want to bring others up with you
Ready to invest 10 months in a real career foundation
requirements:
21 years or older
Full commitment to a 10-month program
Availability for two retreat weekends (winter & summer)
At least one 4–6 week ACTion expedition
Reliable internet and a quiet space for video calls
perks:
Stipend: $8,400 USD over the program
Travel grant: up to $8,000 USD
Two all-expense-paid retreat weekends
Direct mentorship from DAT's Artistic & Executive Directors
Professional development trainings + EDI curriculum
Free tickets to DAT events worldwide
Lifetime DAT alumni network access
Strong letter of recommendation and portfolio of real-world projects
timeline:
Application Deadline :: August 1, 2026
Remote Orientation :: October 7–10, 2026
Remote Work — Fall :: October 11 – December 16, 2026
Independent Project :: December 31 – February 5, 2027
Team Project :: February 6–23, 2027
Pro-Dev Retreat :: February 24–27, 2027
Remote Work — Spring :: February 28 – June 13, 2027
ACTion Expedition :: June 15 – July 25, 2027
faq:
Is this a full-time job? :: No — it's a 20 hrs/wk part-time program. Most apprentices do freelance or part-time work alongside it. We work with you on scheduling.
Is this a path to staff? :: Often, yes. Several current DAT staff started as Apprentices. But the goal is your career, not ours.
When does the next cohort open? :: One Apprentice per season. Apply by August 1 for the Season 19 cohort.
Can international applicants apply? :: Yes. We've had Apprentices from Ecuador, Czechia, Tanzania, and the US.

title: ACTion: Heart of Europe — Ensemble Artists
description: Ensemble artists for our Brno devised theatre project. Experience in physical theatre, devised work, or community-engaged performance preferred. Housing and travel included.
long_description:
DAT's Heart of Europe ACTion takes a small ensemble of artists to Brno, Czechia for four weeks of community-rooted devising. We work with local Roma youth, climate organizers, and Czech theatre-makers to build a new piece from scratch — and we perform it on the last weekend, in a real venue, for the people whose stories we've been working with.

The ensemble is small, mixed, international, and brave. You don't need to speak Czech. You do need to listen.
what_youll_do:
Devise an original piece of theatre over four weeks with the ensemble
Co-lead a community workshop with Roma youth in partnership with ETP Slovensko
Learn local cultural context from Czech artists and historians
Perform the final piece in a public venue in Brno
Document the process for DAT's storytelling channels
who_you_are:
Experience in physical theatre, devised work, ensemble, or community-engaged performance
Comfortable working across language and culture
A listener first, a performer second
Willing to travel internationally and live with the ensemble for 4 weeks
requirements:
18 years or older
Valid passport with 6+ months remaining
US or EU work-eligible (or willingness to handle short-term cultural visa logistics — we help)
Available in-field October 5 – November 1, 2026
perks:
Paid artist fee
Round-trip travel included
Shared ensemble housing in Brno included
Per-diem for meals on workshop days
Documentation footage / photos for your portfolio
Lifetime DAT alumni artist network
timeline:
Application Deadline :: July 1, 2026
Video Auditions :: July 15–25
Callbacks :: August 5–10
Ensemble Announced :: August 20
Remote Pre-Production :: September 1–30, 2026
In-Field Brno :: October 5 – November 1, 2026
faq:
Do I need to speak Czech? :: No. We work in English with translation support.
What does "devised" mean exactly? :: We build the piece together from scratch — no pre-existing script. Bring stories, music, movement, and questions.
Is this safe? :: Yes. We've run ACTions for 15+ years and have deep on-the-ground partnerships in every country we work in.

title: TRAVELOGUE Performers — NYC Season
description: Seeking singers, storytellers, and multi-disciplinary performers for TRAVELOGUE — DAT's interactive travel-storytelling cabaret. Auditions are open to all backgrounds and performance styles.
long_description:
TRAVELOGUE is DAT's signature NYC variety show — part cabaret, part travel journal, part interactive storytelling experiment. Each performance pulls from the stories of DAT artists, alumni, and audience members who have traveled, listened, and brought something back worth singing about.

We're casting a rotating ensemble of 6–8 performers for the upcoming NYC run. Open call — all backgrounds, all training paths, all body types and identities welcome. If you can tell a story and hold a room, we want to hear from you.
what_youll_do:
Perform in a 12-week run at our NYC partner venue
Collaborate on song and storytelling assignments week-to-week
Host segments of the show and improvise with audience contributions
Attend two writing-room sessions per week with DAT artistic team
Optional: join touring versions of TRAVELOGUE in other cities
who_you_are:
Comfortable in cabaret, devised, or storytelling formats
A strong vocalist OR a strong storyteller — we'll cast both
Quick on your feet — improv-friendly
Available for tech week + the full run
requirements:
18 years or older
NYC-local or willing to relocate for the run
Available September 7 – December 5, 2026
Union / Non-Union — both welcome
perks:
Equity-friendly weekly rate
Writing-room credit on new material
Pro-shot reel material from the run
Network night with DAT's NYC artist community
First look at casting calls for the rest of the season
timeline:
Application Deadline :: June 20, 2026
Video Submissions Reviewed :: June 21 – July 5
In-Person Callbacks :: July 18–19 (NYC)
Cast Announced :: August 1
Rehearsals Begin :: August 24, 2026
First Performance :: September 14, 2026
Run :: September 14 – December 5, 2026
faq:
Do I need a song prepared? :: Yes — one song (any style) and a 90-second story.
Is the audition in-person? :: Video first, in-person callbacks in NYC.
Are you Equity? :: Equity-friendly. We work case-by-case for union members.

title: Community Liaison — Bagamoyo
description: Help DAT build authentic community partnerships in coastal Tanzania. Support local outreach, facilitate introductions, and help bridge cultural context for our team on the ground.
long_description:
Bagamoyo is one of DAT's deepest partnership hubs — a coastal Tanzanian town with a long memory and a vibrant arts community. We're looking for a Community Liaison to help our visiting team navigate, listen, and partner well during our late-2026 scouting work.

This role is best suited for someone with local roots in Bagamoyo or East Africa, or someone with strong existing relationships in the region. You'll be the bridge between DAT's visiting team and the artists, organizers, and elders we're meeting with.
what_youll_do:
Facilitate introductions between DAT staff and local partners
Provide cultural context and translation support as needed
Help coordinate logistics on the ground — meetings, transport, hospitality
Co-host one community listening session
Document relationships for our partnership records
who_you_are:
Bagamoyo / East Africa local, OR existing strong relationships in the region
Swahili language ability strongly preferred
Generous, patient, and a natural connector
Comfortable interpreting in formal and informal settings
requirements:
18 years or older
Available on the ground in Bagamoyo for 2–4 weeks between October–December 2026
References from prior community work
Reliable phone and WhatsApp
perks:
Travel support available (case-by-case)
Local per diem for meals during liaison days
Lifetime DAT alumni network access
First right of refusal on future paid Bagamoyo roles
Strong reference letter
timeline:
Application Deadline :: September 1, 2026
Introductory Calls :: September 10–20
Final Liaison Confirmed :: October 1
In-Field :: October – December 2026 (dates flexible)
faq:
Can I apply if I'm not in Tanzania currently? :: If you have deep existing relationships in Bagamoyo, yes. Otherwise, we'd love to talk about other ways to get involved.
Is this a paid role? :: Travel support and per diems, but not a salaried position. We're working to change that.
Will DAT cover my flights? :: Case-by-case. We have a small travel-support fund.

title: Event Support — NYC Hub
description: Support DAT events in New York — front of house, hospitality, setup, and audience experience. A great way to stay close to the work and meet the community.
long_description:
DAT runs 20–30 events a year in NYC — performances, salons, fundraisers, community nights, opening receptions. Behind every one is a team of volunteers who make the room feel like home. This is the entry point for many people into DAT's wider community.

Flexible commitment — show up for one event, or become a regular. Most volunteers do 2–4 events a season.
what_youll_do:
Help with setup and breakdown for events
Greet audiences and check in ticket holders
Support hospitality — drinks, snacks, atmosphere
Help with merch and program sales
Act as audience-experience host during shows
who_you_are:
Friendly, reliable, and easy to be around
Comfortable on your feet for 3–4 hours
Love live performance and want to be near it
requirements:
18 years or older
NYC area
Available for at least one event per quarter (you choose which)
perks:
Complimentary tickets to the event you work
Free tickets to two other DAT events per season
Dinner with the team at quarterly volunteer nights
First look at audition and PLX opportunities
timeline:
Rolling :: Apply anytime
Onboarding :: 30-minute video call within 2 weeks of applying
First Event :: Schedule whenever you're ready
faq:
Do I need to commit to a schedule? :: Nope — you opt into events as they come up.
Can I bring a friend? :: Yes! Just have them apply too.
Is there a minimum age? :: 18+ for events with alcohol service; 16+ otherwise.

title: Development Associate
description: Support fundraising strategy, grant writing, and donor relationships for a growing international arts nonprofit. Ideal for someone with 1–3 years of development experience and a passion for theatre and social impact.
long_description:
DAT is hiring a full-time Development Associate to grow and tend our funding base. You'll work directly with the Executive Director and a small Development team to win grants, deepen donor relationships, and tell DAT's story to people who can help it grow.

We want someone who's hungry to learn, sharp on the page, and genuinely interested in arts funding as a craft. This is a great seat for someone moving from a Development Coordinator role into a more strategic one.
what_youll_do:
Draft and submit 12–15 grant applications per year
Manage the donor CRM (Salesforce NPSP) and donor communications calendar
Draft major-donor proposals and acknowledgement letters
Support the planning and execution of two annual fundraising events
Research new funding prospects (foundations, government, corporate)
Report to funders with clear, story-rich impact narratives
who_you_are:
1–3 years in nonprofit development (or strong grant-writing portfolio)
A confident, clear writer — you can make a budget feel like a story
Organized and deadline-driven
Genuinely curious about international theatre and community work
Generous teammate energy
requirements:
Bachelor's degree or equivalent professional experience
Proficient with Salesforce NPSP or similar CRM (we'll train on ours)
Writing samples (grant or proposal preferred)
US-based (any state) — fully remote
Available for occasional NYC travel
perks:
Salary range: $58,000–$68,000
Full health, dental, vision
401(k) match (up to 4%)
Unlimited PTO
Annual travel to one DAT field expedition (your choice of hub)
Fully remote with quarterly team gatherings
timeline:
Application Deadline :: July 15, 2026
First-Round Video Calls :: July 22 – August 2
Finalist Conversations :: August 10–14
Offer & Start :: September 2026
faq:
Is this truly remote? :: Yes — anywhere in the US. We gather in person quarterly.
Do I need theatre background? :: No, but you should genuinely care about the work. Show it in the application.
Is the salary negotiable? :: Posted range is the range. We're transparent about that.

title: Social Media Coordinator
description: Craft compelling content across DAT's social channels — telling our story to the world. You'll work closely with the producing team and artists to capture and amplify the work in real time.
long_description:
DAT's work happens on five continents. Most of the world will never see it in person. The Social Media Coordinator is how they meet us. You'll work with our producing team and artists to capture the work as it happens, shape it into shareable stories, and grow our community across IG, TikTok, LinkedIn, and YouTube Shorts.

This is a craft role. We're not looking for a metrics jockey — we're looking for a storyteller who's fluent in the language of social.
what_youll_do:
Plan and publish a 4-week content calendar across IG, TikTok, LinkedIn, YouTube Shorts
Direct short-form video shoots (in-house and with field collaborators)
Write captions in DAT's voice
Grow our IG and TikTok followings with intentional, story-led content
Report on reach, engagement, and qualitative resonance quarterly
Collaborate with Development on campaign-tied content
who_you_are:
1–3 years of social-first content experience (agency or in-house)
Good eye, sharp ear, fast hands
Deeply fluent in IG Reels + TikTok grammar
Writing voice that's warm, smart, and not corporate
Fiercely organized about content pipelines
requirements:
Portfolio of social content you've made or led
Proficient with CapCut, Premiere, or DaVinci
US-based (any state) — fully remote, occasional NYC visits
Available for evening/weekend posts around live events
perks:
Hourly rate: $28–32 (commensurate with experience)
Fully remote
Gear stipend for shooting / editing
Annual travel to one DAT field expedition
Flexible schedule outside of event posting windows
timeline:
Application Deadline :: July 1, 2026
Video Calls :: July 8–18
Paid Project Round :: July 22–29
Offer & Start :: August 2026
faq:
Is this a freelance role? :: No — it's part-time W2, 20 hrs/wk.
Will I be the only person on social? :: You'll lead it. The Marketing Manager supports.
Do I need to be on camera? :: Sometimes — for behind-the-scenes content. Not as a full host.

title: ACTion: Quito — Community Storytellers
description: Local Quito community members to share stories and participate in DAT's devised theatre project exploring voice, identity, and place. No performance experience required — your story is enough.
long_description:
DAT returns to Quito for our Season 19 ACTion — and this time the heart of the piece is local voices. We're inviting Quito-based community members to join the storytelling process as collaborators. Share a story from your life. Help us shape it into a scene. Perform it if you want to — or coach the actors who do.

No theatre experience required. Your story is enough.
what_youll_do:
Attend a 2-hour weekly storytelling circle for 6 weeks
Share one or more personal stories with the ensemble (in Spanish, Kichwa, or English)
Help shape how your story becomes a scene
Optional: perform in or co-host the final showing
Receive a copy of the documentation for your own use
who_you_are:
Quito-local (or able to come weekly)
Willing to share a piece of your story with a small group
Curious about how theatre gets made
All ages 16+ welcome
requirements:
16 years or older (under 18 needs parent/guardian sign-off)
Available Tuesday evenings, July 21 – August 25, 2026
Available for final showing weekend August 28–29
Spanish, Kichwa, or English fluency
perks:
Stipend: $200 USD for full participation
Dinner at every storytelling circle
Free tickets to the final showing for your family
Lifetime DAT alumni community access
timeline:
Application Deadline :: July 10, 2026
Welcome Call :: July 14–17
Weekly Circles :: July 21 – August 25, 2026 (Tuesday evenings)
Final Showing :: August 28–29, 2026
faq:
¿Puedo aplicar en español? :: Sí. Bienvenido — escribe en el idioma que prefieras.
Do I have to perform? :: No. Many participants share stories without ever stepping on stage.
Is this a class? :: It's a creative circle — closer to a writing group than a class.

title: Devised Theatre Workshop — Sydney
description: Join DAT artists for a weekend of storytelling and devised theatre exploration in Sydney. Open to all — no experience required. Come ready to play, listen, and make something together.
long_description:
DAT artists are coming to Sydney for one weekend. We're hosting a free, open devised-theatre workshop — bring yourself, bring a story, and we'll make something together in 48 hours.

No experience required. The room will be a mix of working actors, students, community members, and people who haven't been on a stage since Year 9. That's by design.
what_youll_do:
Spend Saturday morning in story-listening circles
Learn DAT's core devising tools through hands-on exercises
Work in small groups to build short scenes from real stories
Share a casual showing for friends and family Sunday evening
Leave with a toolkit you can use anywhere
who_you_are:
Curious — about theatre, about other people's stories, about your own
Willing to be a beginner
Up for two long days in a room with strangers becoming friends
requirements:
16 years or older
Available both Saturday and Sunday of the workshop weekend
Sydney-local or willing to travel
perks:
Workshop is free
Light breakfast + lunch provided both days
Free tickets to any DAT Sydney programming this season
Lifetime DAT alumni community access
timeline:
Sign-Up Opens :: Late August 2026
Workshop Weekend :: TBD (October 2026)
Spaces :: Limited to 24 participants
faq:
When is the exact date? :: Coming soon — sign up to be the first to know.
Is it really free? :: Really.
Can I just come and watch? :: No — this is a participatory room. But you can come to the Sunday showing.

title: General Artist Interest
description: Not seeing the right project right now? Join our artist roster and we'll reach out when the right opportunity comes up. DAT works with artists across disciplines, locations, and career stages.
long_description:
Not seeing the right project right now? Join our artist roster. DAT works with artists across disciplines, locations, and career stages — and we cast from this roster first when new projects open.

Tell us about yourself, what you make, and where you live. We'll reach out when something fits.
what_youll_do:
Submit your bio, headshot/reel, and a short note
Get matched to relevant casting calls and project invitations
Hear from DAT when something fits your profile
who_you_are:
An artist with something to offer — discipline, location, career stage all welcome
Generous, curious, and easy to work with
requirements:
18 years or older
Able to share work samples (links or attachments)
perks:
First look at casting calls and project invitations
Quarterly DAT artist-roster newsletter
Invitations to DAT events near you
timeline:
Rolling :: Submit anytime
Review :: Reviewed quarterly
Matched :: When the right project opens
faq:
Will I hear back even if there's no match? :: We send roster updates quarterly so you know we've got you.
Can I update my submission later? :: Yes — just resubmit with the same email.

title: General Volunteer Interest
description: Want to get involved but not sure where you fit? Tell us about yourself and your skills. We'll match you to opportunities as they come up across all our hubs.
long_description: Want to get involved but not sure where you fit? Tell us about yourself and your skills. We'll match you to opportunities as they come up across all our hubs — events, translation, research, mentorship, advisory, communications, and more.
what_youll_do:
Submit a short interest form
Get a 30-minute call from a DAT team member
Get matched to ongoing or upcoming volunteer needs
who_you_are:
Generous with your time and skills
Open to a range of ways to plug in
perks:
Lifetime DAT volunteer community access
Free tickets to DAT events near you
Quarterly volunteer gatherings
timeline:
Rolling :: Apply anytime
Follow-up Call :: Within 2 weeks
faq:
Is this a real thing or a black hole? :: Real thing. We've matched 200+ volunteers to projects since launching the form.
What if I only have an hour a month? :: That's still useful. Tell us.
